channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden's final foreign trip as first lady will close with her and Trump at Notre Dame cathedral
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden was departing Tuesday on her final solo foreign trip as first lady, a six-day, four-country jaunt through Italy, the United Arab Emirates and Qatar that wraps with her and President-elect Donald Trump joining other dignitaries in Paris to celebrate the reopening of Notre
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy agree on 'need to scrutinize' US funding for NGOs
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy both declare that government funding of non-governmental organizations needs to be scrutinized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump vows to block Japanese steelmaker from buying US Steel, pledges tax incentives and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is underscoring his intention to block the purchase of U.S. Steel by Japanese steelmaker Nippon Steel Corp., and he’s pledging to use tax incentives and tariffs to strengthen the iconic American steelmaker.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy And Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hunter Biden gun case dismissed after President Joe Biden's sweeping pardon
item.description: A federal judge formally dismissed the gun case against Hunter Biden on Tuesday after President Joe Biden issued a sweeping pardon for his son.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge closes Hunter Biden gun case after presidential pardon
item.description: Hunter Biden’s federal gun case was brought to a close by a judge Tuesday following the presidential pardon by his father. In a terse order, U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika terminated all proceedings in the case, citing President Biden’s executive grant of clemency to his son.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: St. Cloud appoints first Somali American to city council
item.description: On a unanimous vote, the city council appointed Hudda Ibrahim to fill a vacancy on the seven-member board. She replaces Jake Anderson, who was elected St. Cloud mayor last month.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: The Associated Press' top photos of the 2024 US election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It's a photograph that defined the 2024 campaign. Donald Trump, campaigning for a second term, raises a fist defiantly toward the sky, blood streaked across his face. But the photo captures just one millisecond of a U.S. presidential campaign unlike any that came before it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump falls just below 50% in popular vote, but gets more than in past elections
item.description: This year's popular-vote margin is the second-closest since 1968 and still tightening. It shows just how closely divided the country is politically, and that any shift to the right is marginal.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Biden says the US is 'all in' on Africa during his Angola visit meant to counter China
item.description: President Joe Biden on Tuesday toured a slavery museum in Angola and inspected shackles and a whip, and he spoke of “our nation’s original sin.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump suggests Canada become 51st state after Trudeau said tariff would kill economy: sources
item.description: President-elect Trump told Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau his country should become the 51st state if it can't fix trade and immigration issues without ripping off the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he'll attend Notre Dame Cathedral reopening celebration in Paris this weekend
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will attend the reopening celebration for Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris this weekend, his first foreign trip since the election.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump taps investment banker Warren Stephens for UK ambassador
item.description: “Warren has always dreamed of serving the United States full time. I am thrilled that he will now have that opportunity as the top Diplomat,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names billionaire investment banker Warren Stephens as his envoy to Britain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has named billionaire investment banker Warren Stephens as his envoy to Britain, a prestigious posting for the Republican donor whose contributions this year included $2 million to a Trump-backing super PAC.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charlamagne tha God knocks Democrats after Hunter Biden pardon
item.description: "I just want Democrats to stop acting like they are on this moral high ground politically, when they have shown us they’re not," he said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Trudeau told Trump Americans would also suffer if tariffs are imposed, a Canadian minister says
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau told Donald Trump that Americans would also suffer if the president-elect follows through on a plan to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a Canadian minister who attended their recent dinner said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Some Democrats are frustrated over Joe Biden reversing course and pardoning his son Hunter
item.description: Already reeling from their November defeat at the polls, Democrats now are grappling with President Joe Biden's pardoning of his son for a federal felony conviction — after the party spent years slamming Donald Trump as a threat to democracy who operates above the law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the rising Democratic populist leaders to watch
item.description: A group of Democrats on the left are searching for a new standard-bearer to put forward an economic populist agenda for America after extensive losses in the November election.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court doesn't seem convinced FDA was unfair in blocking flavored vapes as teen use increased
item.description: A majority of Supreme Court justices didn’t seem convinced Monday that federal regulators misled companies before refusing to allow them to sell sweet flavored vaping products following a surge in teen e-cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader Ben Wikler enters the race to lead the national party
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Ben Wikler, chair of the Democratic Party in Wisconsin, said Sunday he has joined the race to lead the national party after an election that swept Donald Trump and Republicans to power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The billionaires of Trump's proposed administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has a net worth of $5.7 billion, as of Dec. 1, 2024, and he has chosen a slew of fellow billionaires to serve in his second administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Car sales could drop 27% under Trump's plan to eliminate EV tax credit
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has suggested eliminating the federal tax credit for electric vehicles, and some experts expect a drop in EV sales to follow.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens '100% Tariffs' on countries that buck US dollar
item.description: “They can go find another ‘sucker!’ There is no chance that the BRICS will replace the U.S. Dollar in International Trade, and any Country that tries should wave goodbye to America.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump nominates Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: President-elect Trump plans to nominate Kash Patel to serve as head of the FBI, elevating a staunch ally and controversial figure who sparked internal battles during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump wants pardoned real estate developer Charles Kushner to become US ambassador to France
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday he intends to nominate real estate developer Charles Kushner, father of Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, to serve as ambassador to France.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz saga moves toward final act in House
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz’s (R-Fla.) time in Congress may be over, but the saga over the House Ethics Committee’s report on its years-long investigation into him is in its final act.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: NYT publishes email from Pete Hegseth's mom
item.description: The Times noted that Penelope Hegseth described their publishing of the email as "disgusting" and she apologized to her son in a follow-up email.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
